description: A moody, stylized propaganda slogan graphic from the Department of Labor. It is a tightly cropped, heavily processed close-up of a man's face (a duotone/halftone treatment in dusty rose-pink over deep navy blue, with a visible printed dot/grain texture and grunge overlay) showing the eyes, nose, and weathered skin in extreme detail; the subject appears to be a rugged working man, evoking grit and resolve. Centered across the middle in a tall, narrow, slightly glowing white condensed sans-serif typeface (a modern military/industrial 'stencil' style) is the all-caps slogan 'PATRIOTS IN CONTROL.' There is no other text, logo, or seal visible on the image itself. The aesthetic is deliberately cinematic and ominous, resembling a movie-poster teaser. The accompanying tweet text reads 'Patriots are in control, and we're never giving up our fight to put AMERICA FIRST.' Propaganda technique: emotive, masculine, us-in-power imagery designed to project strength and reassurance to supporters that allied 'patriots' now hold institutional power. Target audience is the administration's nationalist base.
